Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Jordan and the lease crisis. The agent uses search_emails with query Jordan lease (or Jordan Ekwueme or 85 Columbia or similar). Discovery: finds Carmen Reyes email from carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com to suki.patel@moveops.com reporting the landlord withdrew the lease at 85 Columbia St Unit 3R after receiving a competing offer at 3200 per month versus the 2700 agreed. The lease was never countersigned. Jordan's household goods are in UrbanNest Cambridge storage. Oakwood temp housing expired April 25. Carmen offers an emergency replacement at 41 Houghton St in Davis Square (Somerville), 1BR at 2650 per month, available immediately, landlord requires signed lease by end of day April 27 (1 day from April 26). Also finds Jordan's panic email from jordan.ekwueme@brightloopanalytics.com (CC tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com) confirming he viewed Davis Square and it looks fine but needs a definitive answer on housing. Also finds Suki's own sent email from April 23 confirming the 85 Columbia apartment to Jordan, establishing the process gap: lease was communicated as confirmed before landlord countersigned.
OE 2: Search emails for BrightLoop to gather the full account communication history. The agent uses search_emails with query BrightLoop (or Tessa Moreno or similar). Discovery: finds approximately 20 emails including Tessa Moreno's original relocation request for Jordan, Mina Hashimoto's welcome response, Jordan's apartment preferences (Cambridge, Somerville, or Brookline, budget 2300-2900, Red Line access, temp housing starting April 22), Suki's apartment request to Carmen, Carmen's three options, Tessa's escalation email to David Chen from April 25 questioning the lease process and linking the outcome to 4-5 future relocations, and correspondence about Simone Richter and Marcus Webb relocations.
OE 3: Search emails for Road Runner to find the vehicle delay notice. The agent uses search_emails with query Road Runner (or Road Runner delay or Marcus Webb car or similar). Discovery: finds the delay notice from dispatch@roadrunnerautotransport.com to suki.patel@moveops.com for Order RR-2026-04-0892, vehicle 2019 Honda Civic VIN 2HGFC2F53KH123456 belonging to Marcus Webb. Picked up Atlanta April 14 as scheduled. Carrier mechanical failure on I-84 near Hartford CT on April 19, vehicle offloaded to Hartford holding facility. Revised ETA April 28-29. Original delivery estimate was April 20, making it 6 days overdue as of April 26. Also finds Marcus Webb's email from marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com asking Suki about his car, noting he has been using Uber since April 20 and wants an update.
OE 4: Search Slack messages for Jordan and BrightLoop to find internal flags. The agent uses conversations_search_messages with search_query Jordan (or BrightLoop or similar). Discovery: finds Chloe Vance's message in channel C006 (operations) doing a status audit on April relocations, calling out four stale Suki records (Daniel Reeves, Maya Johansson, Andre Mitchell, Jordan Ekwueme) and asking about Marcus Webb vehicle shipping. Finds Mina Hashimoto's message in C006 requesting specific status answers from Suki on the Jordan situation (lease countersigned or verbal, Oakwood extended or not, Davis Square locked, plan for stored goods) so she can respond to Tessa and David. Finds David Chen's message in channel C002 (customer-engagement) flagging the BrightLoop escalation, noting the pipeline is worth roughly 28000-35000 in revenue, and requesting a confirmed housing solution for Tessa by end of day.
OE 5: Search Slack messages for Simone Richter to check her rush move status. The agent uses conversations_search_messages with search_query Simone Richter (or Simone BrightLoop or similar). Discovery: finds messages about Simone's rush Chicago-to-Boston move (5-day turnaround, lease end April 6), her 2-week temp housing arrangement, and a complaint that she was placed in a studio instead of the promised 1BR. Mina acknowledged the issue on April 9 but never closed the loop. This means Simone's Airtable record correctly shows In Progress because the housing mismatch is unresolved.
OE 6: Search emails for Simone Richter to verify temp housing and move status. The agent uses search_emails with query Simone Richter (or Simone BrightLoop temp housing or similar). Discovery: finds correspondence about Simone's rush relocation, confirming move dates April 4-6, 2-week furnished temp housing starting approximately April 7. As of April 26 the 2-week window has been over for approximately 6 days. Also finds Simone's email to Mina on April 8 about the studio-vs-1BR mismatch. Disambiguation trap: contacts_search_contacts for Simone Richter returns two results: one at BrightLoop Analytics (simone.richter@brightloopanalytics.com) and one at StormCloud (simone.richter@stormcloud.io). The agent must select the BrightLoop contact.
OE 7: Search CRM for the BrightLoop company record. The agent uses crm_search_companies with name BrightLoop. Discovery: returns company_brightloop (BrightLoop Analytics, Data Analytics, Boston MA, 145 employees, 28 million annual revenue). This company_id is needed for subsequent deal and engagement searches.
OE 8: Search CRM deals for BrightLoop to understand the deal landscape. The agent uses crm_search_deals with company_id company_brightloop (or dealname BrightLoop). Discovery: finds deal_brightloop_q2 (7000, Jordan Ekwueme relocation, stage qualifiedtobuy, BrightLoop first relocation, 4-5 more planned if successful) and deal_brightloop_apr2026 (11350, Simone Richter plus Marcus Webb relocations, stage closedwon, includes Road Runner vehicle shipping at 1100).
OE 9: List CRM engagements for BrightLoop to find account activity notes. The agent uses crm_list_engagements with company_ids containing company_brightloop. Discovery: finds engagement notes about the April relocation wave (Simone rush, Marcus vehicle shipping) and the escalation note from David Chen documenting Tessa's email about the housing failure with the 5 key concerns and the resolution deadline of end of day April 26.
OE 10: Search QuickBooks for BrightLoop invoices to determine billing status. The agent uses quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria referencing BrightLoop (or fetchAll and filters locally). Discovery: finds INV-2026-0305 (10000, 2 standard relocations plus equipment stipend management) and INV-2026-0308 (11350, Simone Richter standard 4500 plus rush surcharge 750, Marcus Webb standard 4500 plus Road Runner vehicle shipping 1100 plus stipend platform fee 500). Jordan Ekwueme's relocation (deal_brightloop_q2, estimated 7000) has not been invoiced yet. The 1100 vehicle shipping charge on INV-2026-0308 should be flagged because the Road Runner delivery has not been completed.
OE 11: Search Airtable for Jordan Ekwueme relocation record. The agent uses airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Name, value Jordan Ekwueme. Discovery: finds recReloc00000008, BrightLoop Analytics, Status Completed, Origin San Diego CA, Destination Boston MA, Move Start April 25, Assigned Coordinator Suki Patel. The Completed status is incorrect given the housing emergency and needs to be changed back to In Progress.
OE 12: Search Airtable for Daniel Reeves relocation record. The agent uses airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Name, value Daniel Reeves. Discovery: finds recReloc00000000031, Vectral Systems, Status In Progress, Origin Seattle WA, Destination San Diego CA, Move Start April 10, Move End April 12, Assigned Coordinator Suki Patel. Move window ended 14 days ago as of April 26 and status needs updating to Completed.
OE 13: Search Airtable for Maya Johansson relocation record. The agent uses airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Name, value Maya Johansson. Discovery: finds recReloc00000000032, Vectral Systems, Status In Progress, Origin Portland OR, Destination San Diego CA, Move Start April 14, Move End April 16, Assigned Coordinator Suki Patel. Move window ended 10 days ago as of April 26 and status needs updating to Completed.
OE 14: Search Airtable for Andre Mitchell relocation record. The agent uses airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Name, value Andre Mitchell. Discovery: finds recReloc00000000038, Mosaic Robotics, Status In Progress, Origin Columbus OH, Destination Boise ID, Move Start April 22, Move End April 24, Assigned Coordinator Suki Patel. Move window ended 2 days ago as of April 26 and status needs updating to Completed.
OE 15: Search Airtable for Simone Richter relocation record. The agent uses airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Name, value Simone Richter. Discovery: finds recSimoneRichterBrightloop, BrightLoop Analytics, Status In Progress, Origin Chicago, Destination Boston, Move Start April 4, Move End April 6, Assigned Coordinator Suki Patel. The record still says In Progress because the move itself completed but the housing type mismatch (studio vs promised 1BR) remains unresolved.
OE 16: Search Airtable for Deshawn Hartley relocation record. The agent uses airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Name, value Deshawn Hartley. Discovery: finds recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver, Mosaic Robotics, Status In Progress, Origin Tampa FL, Destination Denver CO, Move Start April 14, Move End April 19, Assigned Coordinator Suki Patel. The hard deadline was April 19 for the Automate 2026 trade show (April 21-24 at Denver Convention Center, booth setup April 20). Move window ended 7 days ago as of April 26 and record still says In Progress. The agent should verify whether the delivery met the deadline and update accordingly.
OE 17: Search Linear for the Jordan Ekwueme housing emergency tracking ticket. The agent uses linear_list_issues with query Jordan (or Ekwueme or housing emergency or BrightLoop). Discovery: finds linear_issue_inject_ekwueme_housing_001, title URGENT Jordan Ekwueme housing emergency, number 501, team operations, priority urgent, state in progress, assigned to Suki Patel, due April 27. Description contains outstanding items: confirm Jordan approval of Davis Square, extend or replace Oakwood, coordinate goods delivery, update Tessa, review lease process.
OE 18: Search contacts for Carmen Reyes to get her email address. The agent uses contacts_search_contacts with query Carmen. Discovery: returns carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com (Housing Partnerships Manager at UrbanNest Solutions). Disambiguation note: there may be two Carmen Reyes contacts with different phone numbers and locations (New York and Austin). The agent must select the correct one.
OE 19: Search contacts for other key people. The agent uses contacts_search_contacts with query Mina (or Jordan or similar). Discovery: returns mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, jordan.ekwueme@brightloopanalytics.com, and other contacts needed for write actions.
OE 20: List Slack channels to find the channel ID for operations. The agent uses channels_list with channel_types public_channel. Discovery: returns all public channels including C006 (operations). The agent needs this ID to post the situation update.
OE 21 (Write Action): Update the four stale Airtable relocation records plus Deshawn Hartley. The agent uses airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblRelocations01. Updates Daniel Reeves (recReloc00000000031) from In Progress to Completed. Updates Maya Johansson (recReloc00000000032) from In Progress to Completed. Updates Andre Mitchell (recReloc00000000038) from In Progress to Completed. Updates Jordan Ekwueme (recReloc00000008) from Completed back to In Progress with full housing emergency notes. Updates Deshawn Hartley (recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver) from In Progress to Completed with trade show deadline confirmation. The agent may need multiple update calls.
OE 22 (Write Action): Email Carmen Reyes to lock down the Davis Square apartment and arrange bridge housing. The agent uses send_email with sender suki.patel@moveops.com and recipients containing carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com. Content instructs Carmen to proceed with the lease at 41 Houghton St Davis Square, ensure the landlord countersigns before confirming to anyone, arrange 1-2 nights of emergency bridge housing for Jordan tonight and tomorrow, and schedule household goods delivery from Cambridge storage to Davis Square once the lease is executed.
OE 23 (Write Action): Update the Linear tracking ticket with resolution status. The agent uses linear_create_comment with issueId linear_issue_inject_ekwueme_housing_001 (or linear_update_issue with id linear_issue_inject_ekwueme_housing_001). Body contains the resolution actions: Davis Square locked with Carmen, bridge housing arranged, lease countersignature required this time, Mina and David notified, Jordan sent confirmed plan. Notes the process gap: lease was confirmed before landlord countersigned.
OE 24 (Write Action): Let Mina Hashimoto know what is happening with her BrightLoop account. The agent uses send_email with sender suki.patel@moveops.com and recipients containing mina.hashimoto@moveops.com. Content covers the Jordan housing resolution (Davis Square locked, bridge housing for tonight, goods delivery plan), answers Mina's 4 Slack questions (lease was verbal not countersigned, Oakwood expired, Davis Square available, goods in storage), and provides context Mina can relay to Tessa and David.
OE 25 (Write Action): Post the full situation in the operations Slack channel. The agent uses conversations_add_message with channel_id C006 and payload containing the complete Jordan housing crisis briefing for Chloe's Monday ops review: what happened with the lease (never countersigned, pulled for higher offer), resolution plan (Davis Square, bridge housing, goods delivery timeline), all four stale Airtable record updates (Reeves, Johansson, Mitchell to Completed; Jordan to In Progress), Simone Richter status (In Progress correctly due to studio mismatch), Marcus Webb vehicle delay (Hartford, ETA April 28-29), and Deshawn Hartley trade show delivery confirmation.
OE 26 (Write Action): Send Jordan a confirmed housing plan. The agent uses send_email with sender suki.patel@moveops.com and recipients containing jordan.ekwueme@brightloopanalytics.com. Content confirms the Davis Square apartment at 41 Houghton St (2650 per month, 8 min walk to Red Line), provides the timeline for lease signing (by April 27) and goods delivery (targeting April 28-29), explains what happened with 85 Columbia (landlord withdrew, process gap acknowledged), and confirms bridge housing for tonight and tomorrow while the lease is finalized.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Jordan and the lease crisis. The agent uses search_emails with query Jordan lease (or Jordan Ekwueme or 85 Columbia or similar). Discovery: finds Carmen Reyes email from carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com to suki.patel@moveops.com reporting the landlord withdrew the lease at 85 Columbia St Unit 3R after receiving a competing offer at 3200 per month versus the 2700 agreed. The lease was never countersigned. Jordan's household goods are in UrbanNest Cambridge storage. Oakwood temp housing expired April 25. Carmen offers an emergency replacement at 41 Houghton St in Davis Square (Somerville), 1BR at 2650 per month, available immediately, landlord requires signed lease by end of day April 27 (1 day from April 26). Also finds Jordan's panic email from jordan.ekwueme@brightloopanalytics.com (CC tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com) confirming he viewed Davis Square and it looks fine but needs a definitive answer on housing. Discovery: finds recSimoneRichterBrightloop, BrightLoop Analytics, Status In Progress, Origin Chicago, Destination Boston, Move Start April 4, Move End April 6, Assigned Coordinator Suki Patel. The record still says In Progress because the move itself completed but the housing type mismatch (studio vs promised 1BR) remains unresolved.
OE 16: Search Airtable for Deshawn Hartley relocation record. The agent uses airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Name, value Deshawn Hartley. Discovery: finds recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver, Mosaic Robotics, Status In Progress, Origin Tampa FL, Destination Denver CO, Move Start April 14, Move End April 19, Assigned Coordinator Suki Patel. The hard deadline was April 19 for the Automate 2026 trade show (April 21-24 at Denver Convention Center, booth setup April 20). Move window ended 7 days ago as of April 26 and record still says In Progress. The agent should verify whether the delivery met the deadline and update accordingly.
OE 17: Search Linear for the Jordan Ekwueme housing emergency tracking ticket. The agent uses linear_list_issues with query Jordan (or Ekwueme or housing emergency or BrightLoop). Discovery: finds linear_issue_inject_ekwueme_housing_001, title URGENT Jordan Ekwueme housing emergency, number 501, team operations, priority urgent, state in progress, assigned to Suki Patel, due April 27. Description contains outstanding items: confirm Jordan approval of Davis Square, extend or replace Oakwood, coordinate goods delivery, update Tessa, review lease process.
OE 18: Search contacts for Carmen Reyes to get her email address. The agent uses contacts_search_contacts with query Carmen. Discovery: returns carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com (Housing Partnerships Manager at UrbanNest Solutions). Disambiguation note: there may be two Carmen Reyes contacts with different phone numbers and locations (New York and Austin). The agent must select the correct one.
OE 19: Search contacts for other key people. The agent uses contacts_search_contacts with query Mina (or Jordan or similar). Discovery: returns mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, jordan.ekwueme@brightloopanalytics.com, and other contacts needed for write actions.
OE 20: List Slack channels to find the channel ID for operations. The agent uses channels_list with channel_types public_channel. Discovery: returns all public channels including C006 (operations). The agent needs this ID to post the situation update.
OE 21 (Write Action): Update the four stale Airtable relocation records plus Deshawn Hartley. The agent uses airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblRelocations01. Updates Daniel Reeves (recReloc00000000031) from In Progress to Completed. Updates Maya Johansson (recReloc00000000032) from In Progress to Completed. Updates Andre Mitchell (recReloc00000000038) from In Progress to Completed. Updates Jordan Ekwueme (recReloc00000008) from Completed back to In Progress with full housing emergency notes. Updates Deshawn Hartley (recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver) from In Progress to Completed with trade show deadline confirmation. The agent may need multiple update calls.
OE 22 (Write Action): Email Carmen Reyes to lock down the Davis Square apartment and arrange bridge housing. The agent uses send_email with sender suki.patel@moveops.com and recipients containing carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com. Content instructs Carmen to proceed with the lease at 41 Houghton St Davis Square, ensure the landlord countersigns before confirming to anyone, arrange 1-2 nights of emergency bridge housing for Jordan tonight and tomorrow, and schedule household goods delivery from Cambridge storage to Davis Square once the lease is executed.
OE 23 (Write Action): Update the Linear tracking ticket with resolution status. The agent uses linear_create_comment with issueId linear_issue_inject_ekwueme_housing_001 (or linear_update_issue with id linear_issue_inject_ekwueme_housing_001). Body contains the resolution actions: Davis Square locked with Carmen, bridge housing arranged, lease countersignature required this time, Mina and David notified, Jordan sent confirmed plan. Notes the process gap: lease was confirmed before landlord countersigned.
OE 24 (Write Action): Let Mina Hashimoto know what is happening with her BrightLoop account. The agent uses send_email with sender suki.patel@moveops.com and recipients containing mina.hashimoto@moveops.com. Content covers the Jordan housing resolution (Davis Square locked, bridge housing for tonight, goods delivery plan), answers Mina's 4 Slack questions (lease was verbal not countersigned, Oakwood expired, Davis Square available, goods in storage), and provides context Mina can relay to Tessa and David.
OE 25 (Write Action): Post the full situation in the operations Slack channel. The agent uses conversations_add_message with channel_id C006 and payload containing the complete Jordan housing crisis briefing for Chloe's Monday ops review: what happened with the lease (never countersigned, pulled for higher offer), resolution plan (Davis Square, bridge housing, goods delivery timeline), all four stale Airtable record updates (Reeves, Johansson, Mitchell to Completed; Jordan to In Progress), Simone Richter status (In Progress correctly due to studio mismatch), Marcus Webb vehicle delay (Hartford, ETA April 28-29), and Deshawn Hartley trade show delivery confirmation.
OE 26 (Write Action): Send Jordan a confirmed housing plan. The agent uses send_email with sender suki.patel@moveops.com and recipients containing jordan.ekwueme@brightloopanalytics.com. Content confirms the Davis Square apartment at 41 Houghton St (2650 per month, 8 min walk to Red Line), provides the timeline for lease signing (by April 27) and goods delivery (targeting April 28-29), explains what happened with 85 Columbia (landlord withdrew, process gap acknowledged), and confirms bridge housing for tonight and tomorrow while the lease is finalized.
